scenario_description: User asks agent to sort data, then interrupts to create a backup copy of the file, then asks to continue with conditional formatting.

command: DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true
DISPLAY=:1 xdotool key Escape 2>/dev/null; sleep 0.3; DISPLAY=:1 xdotool search --class 'soffice' windowfocus windowraise 2>/dev/null || DISPLAY=:1 wmctrl -xa 'soffice.Soffice' 2>/dev/null || true

phases.instruction: Sort the spreadsheet by the Sales column from highest to lowest
Hold on - before you do anything else, please save a backup copy of this file as WeeklySales_backup.xlsx on the Desktop
Great. Now in the original WeeklySales.xlsx, add a Profit column (Sales minus COGS) and save.

result.command: python3 -c "import pandas as pd,os; f1=os.path.exists('/home/user/Desktop/WeeklySales_backup.xlsx'); df=pd.read_excel('/home/user/Desktop/WeeklySales.xlsx'); print('PASSED' if f1 and 'Profit' in df.columns else 'FAILED')"
